advantages: Expressive statistical plots from DataFrame column names— less indexing boilerplate than raw Matplotlib for common EDA tasks.
Built-in themes, palettes, and regression/confidence-band logic produce informative defaults without manual styling.
Figure-level faceting (relplot, catplot) creates small multiples in one call for comparing groups across dimensions.
Native support for distribution, pair, and correlation heatmap workflows that are awkward to assemble from scratch.
disadvantages: Less flexible than Matplotlib for arbitrary layouts, custom annotations, or non-statistical diagrams (network graphs, custom axes).
Figure-level functions return FacetGrid objects— mixing them with manual subplot grids requires understanding the object model.
Heavy reliance on pandas dtypes; non-tabular or ragged data need reshaping before plotting.
Large pair plots and dense heatmaps become slow and unreadable as feature count grows— not suited for high-dimensional raw feature matrices.
